headline: Menteri Besar Inc companies worth RM28 billion operate without public scrutiny
context: State investment arms known as 'Menteri Besar Incorporated' companies across 11 states manage combined assets of RM28 billion with minimal transparency or accountability requirements.

cards.big: Menteri Besar Inc companies control RM28 billion — most publish no accounts
cards.sub: State leaders' investment vehicles operate as personal corporate fiefdoms

cards.big: MB Inc entities incorporated under Companies Act — exempt from state assembly oversight
cards.sub: Corporate veil shields public assets from democratic accountability

cards.big: Selangor's Permodalan Negeri alone manages RM12 billion in assets
cards.sub: Single state investment arm exceeds entire budget of several smaller states

cards.big: MB Inc boards are appointed solely by the sitting Menteri Besar with no vetting
cards.sub: One individual controls appointment, strategy, and spending of billions in public wealth

cards.big: Public companies controlled by one politician with no oversight aren't state investment — they're personal empires
Should MB Inc companies be subjected to state assembly oversight and public financial reporting?
